title: AP World History: Modern MCQ Practice Exam 06

questions.prompt: Originalized court poem, Majapahit Java, c. 1365: “The ruler’s messengers travel from the rice plains to the island harbors. Lords bring tribute to the capital, priests bless the king’s cosmic order, and ships from many seas anchor where officials weigh pepper and cloth.” What was the most likely purpose of the poem?

choices.A: To portray the ruler as the center of political, religious, and commercial order
choices.B: To argue that Java was isolated from maritime trade
choices.C: To show that priests replaced kings as military commanders
choices.D: To promote the abolition of tribute in Southeast Asia

questions.correctAnswerText: To portray the ruler as the center of political, religious, and commercial order
questions.explanation: The poem links tribute, priests, messengers, officials, harbors, and trade to the ruler’s authority.

questions.prompt: Originalized court poem, Majapahit Java, c. 1365: “The ruler’s messengers travel from the rice plains to the island harbors. Lords bring tribute to the capital, priests bless the king’s cosmic order, and ships from many seas anchor where officials weigh pepper and cloth.” Which phrase best supports the claim that religious ideas helped legitimize kingship?

choices.A: “Ships from many seas”
choices.B: “Officials weigh pepper”
choices.C: “Priests bless the king’s cosmic order”
choices.D: “Rice plains”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Priests bless the king’s cosmic order”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly connects religious authority with royal legitimacy.

questions.prompt: Originalized court poem, Majapahit Java, c. 1365: “The ruler’s messengers travel from the rice plains to the island harbors. Lords bring tribute to the capital, priests bless the king’s cosmic order, and ships from many seas anchor where officials weigh pepper and cloth.” Which broader context best explains the reference to island harbors and overseas ships?

choices.A: The decline of Southeast Asian participation in maritime exchange
choices.B: The importance of Southeast Asian states in regional sea-lane commerce
choices.C: The spread of Atlantic plantation economies into Java before 1450
choices.D: The replacement of all maritime trade by Saharan caravan routes

questions.correctAnswerText: The importance of Southeast Asian states in regional sea-lane commerce
questions.explanation: Harbors, ships, pepper, and cloth show Majapahit’s connection to maritime commerce.

questions.prompt: Originalized court poem, Majapahit Java, c. 1365: “The ruler’s messengers travel from the rice plains to the island harbors. Lords bring tribute to the capital, priests bless the king’s cosmic order, and ships from many seas anchor where officials weigh pepper and cloth.” The passage most directly suggests which social arrangement?

choices.A: A society without elites or specialized officials
choices.B: A hierarchy linking ruler, regional lords, priests, officials, and merchants
choices.C: A society governed entirely by foreign ship captains
choices.D: A political system based only on pastoral nomadism

questions.correctAnswerText: A hierarchy linking ruler, regional lords, priests, officials, and merchants
questions.explanation: The passage describes social and political roles connected to the ruler and state.

questions.prompt: Secondary-source description of Ancestral Pueblo communities in the North American Southwest, c. 1200: “Stone and adobe rooms were built into canyon walls or clustered near seasonal water sources. Storage rooms held maize, beans, and squash, while roads and signal points connected scattered settlements.” Which interpretation is best supported by the description?

choices.A: Pueblo communities adapted architecture and agriculture to arid environments.
choices.B: Pueblo communities depended on imported wheat and sheep before 1450.
choices.C: Pueblo settlements were founded by European missionaries.
choices.D: Pueblo societies avoided all regional communication.

questions.correctAnswerText: Pueblo communities adapted architecture and agriculture to arid environments.
questions.explanation: Canyon-wall or seasonal-water settlements, storage rooms, and maize agriculture show adaptation to dry conditions.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of northern Europe c. 1350 shows sea routes linking Lübeck, Bergen, Novgorod, London, and Bruges. Symbols mark furs and wax moving west, wool cloth moving east, and dried fish moving south from the North Atlantic.” Which conclusion is best supported by the map?

choices.A: Northern European trade networks linked Baltic, North Sea, and Atlantic regions.
choices.B: Trade in northern Europe was limited to local village markets.
choices.C: The Hanseatic cities were isolated from long-distance exchange.
choices.D: Mediterranean ports controlled all commerce in northern Europe.

questions.correctAnswerText: Northern European trade networks linked Baltic, North Sea, and Atlantic regions.
questions.explanation: The map connects ports across the Baltic, North Sea, and Atlantic with multiple commodities.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of northern Europe c. 1350 shows sea routes linking Lübeck, Bergen, Novgorod, London, and Bruges. Symbols mark furs and wax moving west, wool cloth moving east, and dried fish moving south from the North Atlantic.” Which development most directly supported the exchange shown?

choices.A: Steamships and telegraphs
choices.B: Caravanserais across the Sahara
choices.C: Merchant associations, port warehouses, and improved sailing vessels
choices.D: Plantation labor systems in the Caribbean

questions.correctAnswerText: Merchant associations, port warehouses, and improved sailing vessels
questions.explanation: Hanseatic and northern European trade depended on merchant organization, port facilities, and sailing technology.

questions.prompt: Described map: “A map of northern Europe c. 1350 shows sea routes linking Lübeck, Bergen, Novgorod, London, and Bruges. Symbols mark furs and wax moving west, wool cloth moving east, and dried fish moving south from the North Atlantic.” Which map detail best supports the claim that environmental zones shaped trade?

choices.A: Dried fish moving south from the North Atlantic
choices.B: The city of London
choices.C: Sea routes linking ports
choices.D: The date c. 1350

questions.correctAnswerText: Dried fish moving south from the North Atlantic
questions.explanation: Fish exports from the North Atlantic show how regional environments shaped specialized trade.

questions.prompt: Originalized letter from a Jewish merchant in the western Mediterranean, c. 1200: “Send the indigo by the ship from Ifriqiya, and write in Arabic for the customs clerk. My cousin in al-Andalus will receive the goods, but use Hebrew letters so our partners may read the account quickly.” Which audience would most likely have used a document like this?

choices.A: Buddhist monks translating sutras in Central Asia
choices.B: Merchants operating across multilingual Mediterranean communities
choices.C: Officials relocating factories during World War II
choices.D: Peasant rebels demanding land reform

questions.correctAnswerText: Merchants operating across multilingual Mediterranean communities
questions.explanation: The letter’s Arabic, Hebrew letters, Ifriqiya, and al-Andalus references reflect multilingual merchant networks.

questions.prompt: Originalized letter from a Jewish merchant in the western Mediterranean, c. 1200: “Send the indigo by the ship from Ifriqiya, and write in Arabic for the customs clerk. My cousin in al-Andalus will receive the goods, but use Hebrew letters so our partners may read the account quickly.” The letter most directly illustrates which feature of medieval exchange networks?

choices.A: Commercial exchange could encourage multilingual and diasporic cultural practices.
choices.B: Merchants avoided written communication across the Mediterranean.
choices.C: Religious minorities were absent from trade networks.
choices.D: Maritime commerce ended contact between Islamic and European regions.

questions.correctAnswerText: Commercial exchange could encourage multilingual and diasporic cultural practices.
questions.explanation: The letter shows merchants using different languages and scripts across Mediterranean trade communities.

questions.prompt: Originalized Songhai administrative order, c. 1500: “Let the market inspector in Timbuktu record the salt measures and settle disputes among caravan men. The cavalry commanders shall guard the Niger crossings, and the judges shall hear cases according to law. Tribute from the river towns must reach Gao before the rains.” What was the most likely purpose of the order?

choices.A: To coordinate market regulation, military protection, law, and tribute collection
choices.B: To abolish trans-Saharan trade in salt
choices.C: To make Timbuktu independent from Songhai authority
choices.D: To replace cavalry with industrial conscription

questions.correctAnswerText: To coordinate market regulation, military protection, law, and tribute collection
questions.explanation: The order mentions market inspection, salt measures, cavalry protection, judges, tribute, Timbuktu, and Gao.

questions.prompt: Originalized Songhai administrative order, c. 1500: “Let the market inspector in Timbuktu record the salt measures and settle disputes among caravan men. The cavalry commanders shall guard the Niger crossings, and the judges shall hear cases according to law. Tribute from the river towns must reach Gao before the rains.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the state regulated commerce?

choices.A: “Record the salt measures”
choices.B: “Before the rains”
choices.C: “Cavalry commanders”
choices.D: “According to law”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Record the salt measures”
questions.explanation: Recording salt measures shows state regulation of commercial exchange.

questions.prompt: Originalized Songhai administrative order, c. 1500: “Let the market inspector in Timbuktu record the salt measures and settle disputes among caravan men. The cavalry commanders shall guard the Niger crossings, and the judges shall hear cases according to law. Tribute from the river towns must reach Gao before the rains.” Which environmental factor most directly shaped the timing mentioned in the order?

choices.A: Seasonal rainfall affecting river travel, roads, and agricultural cycles
choices.B: Freezing conditions across the Niger River for most of the year
choices.C: Monsoon winds in the South China Sea
choices.D: Volcanic eruptions disrupting Atlantic shipping

questions.correctAnswerText: Seasonal rainfall affecting river travel, roads, and agricultural cycles
questions.explanation: The phrase “before the rains” reflects the importance of seasonal environmental conditions.

questions.prompt: Originalized Songhai administrative order, c. 1500: “Let the market inspector in Timbuktu record the salt measures and settle disputes among caravan men. The cavalry commanders shall guard the Niger crossings, and the judges shall hear cases according to law. Tribute from the river towns must reach Gao before the rains.” The reference to judges in Timbuktu most directly reflects which broader development?

choices.A: The spread of Islamic legal and scholarly institutions in West African cities
choices.B: The elimination of urban learning in the Sahel
choices.C: The replacement of Islamic law by European colonial courts before 1500
choices.D: The decline of written administration under Songhai rule

questions.correctAnswerText: The spread of Islamic legal and scholarly institutions in West African cities
questions.explanation: Judges and Timbuktu reflect Islamic law and scholarship in West African urban centers.

questions.prompt: Described Habsburg imperial procession, Vienna, c. 1700: “A painting shows officials carrying banners labeled Bohemia, Hungary, Austria, and Croatia. Nobles wear different regional costumes while bowing before the emperor, and clerics stand near symbols of Catholic victory over Protestant rebels.” Which interpretation is best supported by the visual description?

choices.A: The Habsburg monarchy ruled a composite empire made up of multiple territories and elites.
choices.B: The Habsburg state eliminated all regional identities before 1700.
choices.C: Habsburg authority depended on Buddhist monastic administration.
choices.D: The painting shows a maritime republic without monarchy.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Habsburg monarchy ruled a composite empire made up of multiple territories and elites.
questions.explanation: The banners, regional costumes, nobles, and emperor show a composite monarchy with multiple territories.

questions.prompt: Described Habsburg imperial procession, Vienna, c. 1700: “A painting shows officials carrying banners labeled Bohemia, Hungary, Austria, and Croatia. Nobles wear different regional costumes while bowing before the emperor, and clerics stand near symbols of Catholic victory over Protestant rebels.” Which feature most directly shows that religion was connected to imperial legitimacy?

choices.A: Regional costumes
choices.B: Banners labeled with territories
choices.C: Clerics near symbols of Catholic victory
choices.D: Nobles bowing before the emperor

questions.correctAnswerText: Clerics near symbols of Catholic victory
questions.explanation: The clerics and Catholic victory symbols connect religion to imperial legitimacy.

questions.prompt: Described Habsburg imperial procession, Vienna, c. 1700: “A painting shows officials carrying banners labeled Bohemia, Hungary, Austria, and Croatia. Nobles wear different regional costumes while bowing before the emperor, and clerics stand near symbols of Catholic victory over Protestant rebels.” Which argument is best supported by the image description?

choices.A: Composite empires could display unity while preserving visible regional distinctions.
choices.B: Early modern empires always erased noble status.
choices.C: Religious conflict had no effect on European state-building.
choices.D: Regional elites governed without reference to monarchy.

questions.correctAnswerText: Composite empires could display unity while preserving visible regional distinctions.
questions.explanation: The image shows many territories and costumes unified around the emperor.

questions.prompt:
Table: St. Lawrence fur trade, simplified pattern
Participant | Goods or role
Indigenous hunters and middlemen | Beaver pelts, geographic knowledge, diplomacy
French traders | Metal tools, textiles, firearms, credit
Missionaries and officials | Alliances, conversion efforts, regulation
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The fur trade depended on cooperation, exchange, and negotiation between French and Indigenous groups.
choices.B: French traders controlled all inland territory without Indigenous assistance.
choices.C: Beaver pelts had no role in Atlantic commerce.
choices.D: Missionaries and officials avoided involvement in trade alliances.

questions.correctAnswerText: The fur trade depended on cooperation, exchange, and negotiation between French and Indigenous groups.
questions.explanation: The table lists Indigenous hunters and middlemen, French traders, missionaries, officials, goods, diplomacy, and regulation.

questions.prompt:
Table: St. Lawrence fur trade, simplified pattern
Participant | Goods or role
Indigenous hunters and middlemen | Beaver pelts, geographic knowledge, diplomacy
French traders | Metal tools, textiles, firearms, credit
Missionaries and officials | Alliances, conversion efforts, regulation
Which environmental consequence was most directly connected to the trade pattern shown?

choices.A: The end of animal hunting in North America
choices.B: The spread of rice cultivation into the Arctic
choices.C: Increased pressure on beaver populations in traded regions
choices.D: The disappearance of forest ecosystems before 1500

questions.correctAnswerText: Increased pressure on beaver populations in traded regions
questions.explanation: Demand for beaver pelts increased hunting pressure on beaver populations.

questions.prompt:
Table: St. Lawrence fur trade, simplified pattern
Participant | Goods or role
Indigenous hunters and middlemen | Beaver pelts, geographic knowledge, diplomacy
French traders | Metal tools, textiles, firearms, credit
Missionaries and officials | Alliances, conversion efforts, regulation
Which evidence best supports the claim that cultural interaction accompanied economic exchange?

choices.A: “Beaver pelts”
choices.B: “Metal tools”
choices.C: “Conversion efforts”
choices.D: “Credit”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Conversion efforts”
questions.explanation: Missionary conversion efforts show cultural and religious interaction accompanying trade.

questions.prompt:
Table: St. Lawrence fur trade, simplified pattern
Participant | Goods or role
Indigenous hunters and middlemen | Beaver pelts, geographic knowledge, diplomacy
French traders | Metal tools, textiles, firearms, credit
Missionaries and officials | Alliances, conversion efforts, regulation
Which argument is best supported by the table?

choices.A: European empires in North America always operated independently of Indigenous political relationships.
choices.B: Early colonial power in the St. Lawrence region depended partly on alliances and Indigenous knowledge.
choices.C: Indigenous peoples were excluded from all Atlantic exchange.
choices.D: French colonial officials rejected diplomacy as a tool of empire.

questions.correctAnswerText: Early colonial power in the St. Lawrence region depended partly on alliances and Indigenous knowledge.
questions.explanation: Indigenous geographic knowledge, diplomacy, and alliances were central to French activity.

questions.prompt: Originalized Portuguese naval instruction, western Indian Ocean, c. 1520: “No ship shall pass without a written license bearing the captain’s seal. Those who refuse inspection will have their cargo seized. Fort commanders must report which rulers accept our passes and which send pilots to rival harbors.” What was the most likely purpose of the instruction?

choices.A: To regulate maritime movement and enforce Portuguese commercial authority
choices.B: To abolish all Indian Ocean trade
choices.C: To surrender Portuguese forts to local merchants
choices.D: To encourage unrestricted trade by rival powers

questions.correctAnswerText: To regulate maritime movement and enforce Portuguese commercial authority
questions.explanation: The instruction requires licenses, inspections, cargo seizure, and reporting by fort commanders.

questions.prompt: Originalized Portuguese naval instruction, western Indian Ocean, c. 1520: “No ship shall pass without a written license bearing the captain’s seal. Those who refuse inspection will have their cargo seized. Fort commanders must report which rulers accept our passes and which send pilots to rival harbors.” The system described depended most directly on which development?

choices.A: Steam-powered ironclads
choices.B: Armed ocean-going ships and fortified coastal posts
choices.C: Railroads linking mines to ports
choices.D: Wireless radio communication

questions.correctAnswerText: Armed ocean-going ships and fortified coastal posts
questions.explanation: The Portuguese cartaz system relied on armed ships and forts to police maritime commerce.

questions.prompt: Originalized Portuguese naval instruction, western Indian Ocean, c. 1520: “No ship shall pass without a written license bearing the captain’s seal. Those who refuse inspection will have their cargo seized. Fort commanders must report which rulers accept our passes and which send pilots to rival harbors.” The instruction most directly reflects which broader process?

choices.A: European attempts to redirect and tax existing Asian maritime trade
choices.B: The collapse of all commerce between Africa and Asia
choices.C: The decline of state involvement in maritime exchange
choices.D: The end of competition among Indian Ocean port cities

questions.correctAnswerText: European attempts to redirect and tax existing Asian maritime trade
questions.explanation: The instruction shows Portuguese efforts to regulate and profit from established Indian Ocean trade.

questions.prompt: Originalized imperial edict, Russia, 1861: “The peasant shall no longer be the property of the landlord, yet the village commune shall answer for payments and order. Land is granted by law, but obligations must be fulfilled so that freedom does not become disorder.” What was the most likely purpose of the edict?

choices.A: To emancipate serfs while preserving state control, land payments, and rural order
choices.B: To restore serfdom after the Russian Revolution
choices.C: To abolish village communes and create universal suffrage
choices.D: To make Russia a British colony

questions.correctAnswerText: To emancipate serfs while preserving state control, land payments, and rural order
questions.explanation: The edict grants legal freedom while preserving payments, commune responsibility, and order.

questions.prompt: Originalized imperial edict, Russia, 1861: “The peasant shall no longer be the property of the landlord, yet the village commune shall answer for payments and order. Land is granted by law, but obligations must be fulfilled so that freedom does not become disorder.” Which phrase best supports the claim that emancipation remained limited by continuing obligations?

choices.A: “No longer be the property”
choices.B: “The peasant”
choices.C: “Obligations must be fulfilled”
choices.D: “Granted by law”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Obligations must be fulfilled”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly shows continuing duties after emancipation.

questions.prompt: Originalized imperial edict, Russia, 1861: “The peasant shall no longer be the property of the landlord, yet the village commune shall answer for payments and order. Land is granted by law, but obligations must be fulfilled so that freedom does not become disorder.” Which broader context best explains the edict?

choices.A: Russian leaders sought reform after military weakness and social pressures exposed the limits of serfdom.
choices.B: Russia had fully industrialized before all western European states.
choices.C: Serfdom had disappeared from Russia by 1500.
choices.D: Russian rulers rejected all state-led reform after 1750.

questions.correctAnswerText: Russian leaders sought reform after military weakness and social pressures exposed the limits of serfdom.
questions.explanation: Serf emancipation followed pressures for modernization and reform, especially after military weakness became clear.

questions.prompt: Originalized imperial edict, Russia, 1861: “The peasant shall no longer be the property of the landlord, yet the village commune shall answer for payments and order. Land is granted by law, but obligations must be fulfilled so that freedom does not become disorder.” Which statement best evaluates the reform described?

choices.A: It immediately created complete political equality for peasants.
choices.B: It ended landlord property claims without changing rural society.
choices.C: It expanded legal freedom but preserved state supervision and economic burdens.
choices.D: It rejected the idea that peasants should have any legal rights.

questions.correctAnswerText: It expanded legal freedom but preserved state supervision and economic burdens.
questions.explanation: The reform ended personal ownership but retained village responsibility, payments, and state order.

questions.prompt: Originalized newspaper editorial, Cairo, c. 1881: “A nation cannot be strong when its officers are dismissed for foreign favorites and its peasants pay taxes to satisfy bondholders abroad. A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers, for knowledge and consultation are not rebellion.” Which audience was the editorial most likely intended to influence?

choices.A: Egyptian readers and officials debating constitutional reform and foreign influence
choices.B: Japanese soldiers occupying Southeast Asia
choices.C: Mongol envoys crossing Central Asia
choices.D: European settlers claiming vineyard land in Algeria

questions.correctAnswerText: Egyptian readers and officials debating constitutional reform and foreign influence
questions.explanation: The editorial addresses Egyptian national strength, foreign favorites, bondholders, taxation, and a representative chamber.

questions.prompt: Originalized newspaper editorial, Cairo, c. 1881: “A nation cannot be strong when its officers are dismissed for foreign favorites and its peasants pay taxes to satisfy bondholders abroad. A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers, for knowledge and consultation are not rebellion.” Which phrase best supports the claim that the author favored representative oversight?

choices.A: “Foreign favorites”
choices.B: “Peasants pay taxes”
choices.C: “A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers”
choices.D: “Bondholders abroad”

questions.correctAnswerText: “A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly supports representative oversight.

questions.prompt: Originalized newspaper editorial, Cairo, c. 1881: “A nation cannot be strong when its officers are dismissed for foreign favorites and its peasants pay taxes to satisfy bondholders abroad. A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers, for knowledge and consultation are not rebellion.” The editorial most directly links political reform to which economic grievance?

choices.A: Foreign debt and taxation pressures on Egyptian society
choices.B: The abolition of all export agriculture
choices.C: The collapse of Mediterranean trade after 1200
choices.D: The refusal of Egyptians to use newspapers

questions.correctAnswerText: Foreign debt and taxation pressures on Egyptian society
questions.explanation: The editorial connects peasant taxes and foreign bondholders to political reform.

questions.prompt: Originalized newspaper editorial, Cairo, c. 1881: “A nation cannot be strong when its officers are dismissed for foreign favorites and its peasants pay taxes to satisfy bondholders abroad. A chamber chosen by Egyptians must watch the ministers, for knowledge and consultation are not rebellion.” Which statement best evaluates the editorial’s argument?

choices.A: It rejects public debate as dangerous to national strength.
choices.B: It uses print-based political argument to connect national dignity, economic grievance, and constitutional oversight.
choices.C: It defends foreign financial control as necessary for independence.
choices.D: It argues that peasants should have no role in national politics.

questions.correctAnswerText: It uses print-based political argument to connect national dignity, economic grievance, and constitutional oversight.
questions.explanation: The editorial links foreign influence, taxation, consultation, knowledge, and a chosen chamber.

questions.prompt: Originalized diplomatic letter from Emperor Menelik II of Ethiopia, c. 1896: “We welcome friendship and trade, but no treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection. Our envoys will answer Europe’s kings as equals, and our soldiers will defend the mountains if words are twisted against us.” What was the most likely purpose of the letter?

choices.A: To accept formal colonial protectorate status
choices.B: To defend Ethiopian sovereignty against imperial claims
choices.C: To invite European settlers to govern Ethiopian provinces
choices.D: To reject all diplomacy with foreign states

questions.correctAnswerText: To defend Ethiopian sovereignty against imperial claims
questions.explanation: Menelik rejects protectorate status while allowing friendship and trade.

questions.prompt: Originalized diplomatic letter from Emperor Menelik II of Ethiopia, c. 1896: “We welcome friendship and trade, but no treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection. Our envoys will answer Europe’s kings as equals, and our soldiers will defend the mountains if words are twisted against us.” Which phrase best supports the claim that Menelik rejected protectorate status?

choices.A: “Friendship and trade”
choices.B: “Europe’s kings”
choices.C: “No treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection”
choices.D: “Our soldiers”

questions.correctAnswerText: “No treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly rejects protectorate status.

questions.prompt: Originalized diplomatic letter from Emperor Menelik II of Ethiopia, c. 1896: “We welcome friendship and trade, but no treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection. Our envoys will answer Europe’s kings as equals, and our soldiers will defend the mountains if words are twisted against us.” Which broader context best explains Ethiopia’s ability to resist colonization more successfully than many African states?

choices.A: Ethiopia combined diplomacy, arms purchases, and military organization during the age of imperialism.
choices.B: Ethiopia avoided all contact with European states before 1900.
choices.C: Ethiopia was protected by membership in the United Nations.
choices.D: Ethiopia depended on Japanese military occupation for independence.

questions.correctAnswerText: Ethiopia combined diplomacy, arms purchases, and military organization during the age of imperialism.
questions.explanation: Ethiopia resisted colonization through diplomacy, military preparation, and strategic modernization.

questions.prompt: Originalized diplomatic letter from Emperor Menelik II of Ethiopia, c. 1896: “We welcome friendship and trade, but no treaty shall make Ethiopia a child under another’s protection. Our envoys will answer Europe’s kings as equals, and our soldiers will defend the mountains if words are twisted against us.” Which statement best evaluates the argument in the letter?

choices.A: Menelik argues that trade and diplomacy are compatible with political independence.
choices.B: Menelik claims that Ethiopia should withdraw from all international contact.
choices.C: Menelik accepts racial hierarchy as the basis of imperial diplomacy.
choices.D: Menelik argues that treaties should be interpreted only by European officials.

questions.correctAnswerText: Menelik argues that trade and diplomacy are compatible with political independence.
questions.explanation: The letter welcomes friendship and trade while firmly rejecting subordination.

questions.prompt: Described political cartoon, Iran, 1891: “A tobacco pipe shaped like a foreign company contract stretches across a bazaar. Merchants, clerics, and women pull at the pipe while a court official tries to protect it. The caption reads, ‘Smoke for them, hunger for us.’” Which interpretation is best supported by the cartoon?

choices.A: Iranian society uniformly supported foreign concessions.
choices.B: Opposition to foreign economic concessions could unite merchants, religious leaders, and urban consumers.
choices.C: Tobacco concessions ended all market activity in Iran.
choices.D: Foreign companies avoided influence over domestic economic life.

questions.correctAnswerText: Opposition to foreign economic concessions could unite merchants, religious leaders, and urban consumers.
questions.explanation: The cartoon shows merchants, clerics, and women jointly resisting a foreign contract.

questions.prompt: Described political cartoon, Iran, 1891: “A tobacco pipe shaped like a foreign company contract stretches across a bazaar. Merchants, clerics, and women pull at the pipe while a court official tries to protect it. The caption reads, ‘Smoke for them, hunger for us.’” Which detail most directly supports the claim that resistance crossed social groups?

choices.A: A tobacco pipe appears in the cartoon.
choices.B: The caption mentions hunger.
choices.C: Merchants, clerics, and women all pull at the pipe.
choices.D: A court official protects the contract.

questions.correctAnswerText: Merchants, clerics, and women all pull at the pipe.
questions.explanation: The detail shows participation by several social groups.

questions.prompt: Described political cartoon, Iran, 1891: “A tobacco pipe shaped like a foreign company contract stretches across a bazaar. Merchants, clerics, and women pull at the pipe while a court official tries to protect it. The caption reads, ‘Smoke for them, hunger for us.’” The cartoon most directly connects anti-imperial protest to which process?

choices.A: The granting of economic concessions to foreign firms
choices.B: The abolition of all cash-crop agriculture
choices.C: The decline of urban markets after 1450
choices.D: The end of global capitalism in the nineteenth century

questions.correctAnswerText: The granting of economic concessions to foreign firms
questions.explanation: The cartoon’s foreign company contract symbolizes economic concessions that provoked protest.

questions.prompt: Described political cartoon, Iran, 1891: “A tobacco pipe shaped like a foreign company contract stretches across a bazaar. Merchants, clerics, and women pull at the pipe while a court official tries to protect it. The caption reads, ‘Smoke for them, hunger for us.’” Which argument is best supported by the cartoon?

choices.A: Anti-imperial resistance could be expressed through urban public culture and economic symbolism.
choices.B: Religious leaders never participated in protests against foreign influence.
choices.C: Foreign concessions were accepted because they lowered food prices for all groups.
choices.D: Political cartoons were unrelated to nineteenth-century reform movements.

questions.correctAnswerText: Anti-imperial resistance could be expressed through urban public culture and economic symbolism.
questions.explanation: The cartoon uses bazaar imagery, tobacco symbolism, and social groups to criticize foreign influence.

questions.prompt: Originalized testimony from a Jewish resident of occupied Poland, c. 1942: “The new order says our street is now closed. We wear marks on our coats, surrender radios, and wait for lists posted by officials. Some say the trains go to labor, but families who leave do not write back.” What was the most likely purpose of preserving this testimony?

choices.A: To document the experiences of persecution, segregation, and deportation under Nazi rule
choices.B: To show that Nazi occupation protected religious minorities
choices.C: To praise forced relocation as voluntary labor migration
choices.D: To prove that civilians were not targeted during World War II

questions.correctAnswerText: To document the experiences of persecution, segregation, and deportation under Nazi rule
questions.explanation: The testimony describes closed streets, required markings, confiscation, official lists, and deportation.

questions.prompt: Originalized testimony from a Jewish resident of occupied Poland, c. 1942: “The new order says our street is now closed. We wear marks on our coats, surrender radios, and wait for lists posted by officials. Some say the trains go to labor, but families who leave do not write back.” Which phrase most directly supports the claim that the occupation imposed social segregation?

choices.A: “Our street is now closed”
choices.B: “Surrender radios”
choices.C: “Lists posted by officials”
choices.D: “Do not write back”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Our street is now closed”
questions.explanation: Closing the street shows imposed segregation of a targeted community.

questions.prompt: Originalized testimony from a Jewish resident of occupied Poland, c. 1942: “The new order says our street is now closed. We wear marks on our coats, surrender radios, and wait for lists posted by officials. Some say the trains go to labor, but families who leave do not write back.” Which statement best evaluates the testimony’s historical value?

choices.A: It is valuable because it shows how official language and bureaucratic rules masked escalating persecution.
choices.B: It is useless because personal testimony cannot reveal state policy.
choices.C: It proves that deportees were sent only to ordinary wage labor.
choices.D: It shows that Nazi rule avoided identification and registration systems.

questions.correctAnswerText: It is valuable because it shows how official language and bureaucratic rules masked escalating persecution.
questions.explanation: The testimony reveals how orders, markings, lists, and labor claims accompanied persecution and deportation.

questions.prompt:
Table: Spanish Civil War international involvement, simplified pattern
Actor | Support given
Germany and Italy | Aircraft, pilots, equipment to Nationalists
Soviet Union | Weapons and advisers to Republicans
International Brigades | Volunteers from many countries to Republicans
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: The Spanish Civil War became a testing ground for international ideological and military involvement.
choices.B: The Spanish Civil War involved only local Spanish forces.
choices.C: Foreign powers avoided using modern weapons in the conflict.
choices.D: The conflict ended ideological divisions in Europe.

questions.correctAnswerText: The Spanish Civil War became a testing ground for international ideological and military involvement.
questions.explanation: The table shows fascist, communist, and international volunteer involvement using aircraft, weapons, advisers, and volunteers.

questions.prompt:
Table: Spanish Civil War international involvement, simplified pattern
Actor | Support given
Germany and Italy | Aircraft, pilots, equipment to Nationalists
Soviet Union | Weapons and advisers to Republicans
International Brigades | Volunteers from many countries to Republicans
Which broader context best explains the involvement shown?

choices.A: The disappearance of fascist and communist ideologies after World War I
choices.B: The end of European political polarization during the interwar period
choices.C: The ideological conflicts among fascism, communism, and liberal democracy before World War II
choices.D: The creation of postwar decolonization movements in Asia

questions.correctAnswerText: The ideological conflicts among fascism, communism, and liberal democracy before World War II
questions.explanation: Foreign intervention in Spain reflected broader interwar ideological conflict.

questions.prompt: Originalized speech by Patrice Lumumba, Congo, 1960: “Independence is not a gift wrapped in a flag. It must mean that our copper, our courts, and our soldiers answer to the Congolese people. We ask friendship from abroad, but not masters with new names.” What was the most likely purpose of the speech?

choices.A: To defend continued Belgian rule over Congo
choices.B: To reject economic and political neo-colonial control after independence
choices.C: To support rule by foreign mining companies
choices.D: To argue that independence should exclude control over resources

questions.correctAnswerText: To reject economic and political neo-colonial control after independence
questions.explanation: Lumumba argues that independence must include control over resources, courts, and soldiers.

questions.prompt: Originalized speech by Patrice Lumumba, Congo, 1960: “Independence is not a gift wrapped in a flag. It must mean that our copper, our courts, and our soldiers answer to the Congolese people. We ask friendship from abroad, but not masters with new names.” Which phrase best supports the claim that resource sovereignty was central to the speaker’s argument?

choices.A: “Gift wrapped in a flag”
choices.B: “Our copper”
choices.C: “Friendship from abroad”
choices.D: “Our soldiers”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Our copper”
questions.explanation: Copper directly represents resource sovereignty.

questions.prompt: Originalized speech by Patrice Lumumba, Congo, 1960: “Independence is not a gift wrapped in a flag. It must mean that our copper, our courts, and our soldiers answer to the Congolese people. We ask friendship from abroad, but not masters with new names.” Which statement best evaluates the speaker’s argument?

choices.A: The speaker defines independence as formal symbolism without institutional change.
choices.B: The speaker argues that sovereignty requires control over resources, law, and military power.
choices.C: The speaker rejects all international relationships.
choices.D: The speaker claims that foreign companies should govern postcolonial states.

questions.correctAnswerText: The speaker argues that sovereignty requires control over resources, law, and military power.
questions.explanation: The speech names copper, courts, and soldiers as institutions that must answer to Congolese people.

questions.prompt: Described Chilean campaign poster, early 1970s: “A miner, a student, and a mother stand beneath the words ‘Bread, Copper, and Democracy.’ In the background, a factory chimney and ballot box appear side by side. Smaller text calls for national control of resources through constitutional government.” Which interpretation is best supported by the poster?

choices.A: Cold War-era politics in Latin America could link social welfare, elections, and control of natural resources.
choices.B: Chilean politics rejected the use of electoral campaigns.
choices.C: Resource nationalism had no relationship to democratic politics.
choices.D: The poster supports foreign military rule over Chilean copper.

questions.correctAnswerText: Cold War-era politics in Latin America could link social welfare, elections, and control of natural resources.
questions.explanation: Bread, copper, democracy, a ballot box, and national control of resources link welfare, elections, and resource politics.

questions.prompt: Described Chilean campaign poster, early 1970s: “A miner, a student, and a mother stand beneath the words ‘Bread, Copper, and Democracy.’ In the background, a factory chimney and ballot box appear side by side. Smaller text calls for national control of resources through constitutional government.” The poster most directly reflects which broader Cold War-era pattern?

choices.A: The decline of all ideological conflict in Latin America
choices.B: The use of mass political imagery to mobilize support for competing development visions
choices.C: The disappearance of labor and student activism
choices.D: The replacement of electoral politics by hereditary monarchy

questions.correctAnswerText: The use of mass political imagery to mobilize support for competing development visions
questions.explanation: The poster uses workers, students, mothers, copper, democracy, and industrial imagery to mobilize voters around a development program.

questions.prompt:
Table: Smallpox cases reported globally, simplified pattern
Year | Reported cases
1967 | About 10–15 million
1975 | Fewer than 50,000
1980 | 0 naturally occurring cases certified
Which conclusion is best supported by the table?

choices.A: Global vaccination campaigns helped eliminate naturally occurring smallpox.
choices.B: Smallpox cases increased after 1967.
choices.C: Disease eradication required no international coordination.
choices.D: Smallpox was limited to one region throughout the twentieth century.

questions.correctAnswerText: Global vaccination campaigns helped eliminate naturally occurring smallpox.
questions.explanation: Reported cases fell from millions to zero certified naturally occurring cases by 1980.

questions.prompt: Originalized public health report, World Health Organization field team, late 1970s: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams mark each case on a district map. Vaccinators travel by jeep, boat, and bicycle, and neighbors are asked to report fever and rash quickly.” What was the most likely purpose of the report?

choices.A: To describe coordinated surveillance and vaccination methods used in disease eradication
choices.B: To argue that public health should avoid local cooperation
choices.C: To promote the spread of smallpox as a military weapon
choices.D: To prove that international organizations could not work with villages

questions.correctAnswerText: To describe coordinated surveillance and vaccination methods used in disease eradication
questions.explanation: The report describes case mapping, vaccinators, local cooperation, and rapid reporting.

questions.prompt: Originalized public health report, World Health Organization field team, late 1970s: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams mark each case on a district map. Vaccinators travel by jeep, boat, and bicycle, and neighbors are asked to report fever and rash quickly.” Which phrase best supports the claim that eradication depended on cooperation between global and local actors?

choices.A: “Travel by jeep”
choices.B: “Fever and rash”
choices.C: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams”
choices.D: “District map”

questions.correctAnswerText: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams”
questions.explanation: This phrase directly shows cooperation between local and global actors.

questions.prompt: Originalized public health report, World Health Organization field team, late 1970s: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams mark each case on a district map. Vaccinators travel by jeep, boat, and bicycle, and neighbors are asked to report fever and rash quickly.” The campaign described most directly reflects which broader feature of globalization?

choices.A: The elimination of all disease through private trade alone
choices.B: The use of transnational institutions and technology to address shared human health risks
choices.C: The decline of scientific knowledge in public policy
choices.D: The isolation of rural communities from global institutions

questions.correctAnswerText: The use of transnational institutions and technology to address shared human health risks
questions.explanation: WHO teams, maps, transport, vaccination, and local reporting reflect global health cooperation.

questions.prompt: Originalized public health report, World Health Organization field team, late 1970s: “Local health workers, village leaders, and international teams mark each case on a district map. Vaccinators travel by jeep, boat, and bicycle, and neighbors are asked to report fever and rash quickly.” Which statement best evaluates the report’s argument?

choices.A: It presents eradication as a purely technological process without social participation.
choices.B: It shows that global campaigns worked only when local knowledge and community reporting were incorporated.
choices.C: It argues that maps were unnecessary for disease control.
choices.D: It claims that international teams replaced all local authority.

questions.correctAnswerText: It shows that global campaigns worked only when local knowledge and community reporting were incorporated.
questions.explanation: The report emphasizes village leaders, local health workers, neighbors, maps, and international teams working together.
